mnemonics: VEGAS: Vanishing/Exploding Gradients, Overfitting, Initialization, Activation functions, Saddle points
keyPoints: Vanishing and Exploding Gradients make training slow or unstable
Overfitting occurs when the model memorizes noise instead of learning patterns
Poor weight initialization and choice of activation function can hinder learning
Optimizers can get stuck in saddle points, which are flat regions in the loss landscape
commonMistakes: Using the same learning rate for all parameters and throughout training
Initializing all weights to zero, which prevents neurons from learning differently
Not using techniques like dropout or regularization to prevent overfitting
Choosing an activation function without considering the vanishing gradient problem (e.g., sigmoid)
